stage.title: Capability + topology resolution
stage.objective: Resolve everything the later stages need to stay portable: (a) preflight the invocation (scope-oriented — $ARGUMENTS is the OPTIONAL review scope, NOT a required description), (b) detect capability SIGNALS, (c) resolve the project TOPOLOGY and quality gates from the rules catalogue, and (d) emit a single resolved context block the downstream stages consume. ZERO project-shaped value is hardcoded — every one is resolved from `tmux-cli rules resolve` / `--signals` / manifests; greenfield skeleton values are DEFAULTS only.

substep.title: Preflight (scope-oriented)
substep.action: Tokenize $ARGUMENTS into the OPTIONAL review scope. Trim whitespace. UNLIKE /tmux:feature, empty $ARGUMENTS is NOT a hard stop: empty → the default working-tree-vs-HEAD scope (resolved fully in Stage 1). Record the raw scope token (empty / git-range / PR ref / explicit paths) for Stage 1 to resolve into `tmux-cli rules check` flags.
Confirm the tmux-cli MCP precondition by calling a read-only MCP tool (e.g. windows-list).

check.action: Print exactly: "ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry."

substep.action: Read .tmux-cli/setting.yaml from the project root (model the read on task-list.xml step 0). Record whether the file is present; capture any settings the later stages reference (the confirm gate reads `cr.auto_approve`, falling back to `feature.auto_approve`). It is NOT required to exist — record "absent" and proceed if missing.

substep.title: Capability signal resolution
substep.action: Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --signals --json`. This short-circuits before the manifest load and ALWAYS exits 0 on a valid project (greenfield included, emitting unknowns). Parse the printed JSON object.

Extract these SIGNALS fields verbatim from the JSON: `lang`, `framework`, `run_target`, `has_database`, `has_frontend`, `frontend_mode`, `n_auth_flows`. (Carry forward uses_jwt / has_mailer / has_messenger / has_http_client / n_bounded_contexts too if present, but the seven above are the required set.)

check.action: Treat as a genuine tool defect — follow the shared error-reporting procedure (referenced from cr-apply-fixes.xml's <execution-rules>), then STOP.

substep.action: Identify UNKNOWN signals using the zero-value convention: empty string for `lang` / `framework` / `run_target` / `frontend_mode`; the literal "unknown" for the Tri fields `has_database` / `has_frontend`; `n_auth_flows == -1`. AUTONOMOUS context (cr-apply-fixes never blocks on the user here): apply the greenfield DEFAULT and record the default + rationale — `lang`/`framework` = leave as detected/empty (do not invent a stack); `run_target` = "local"; `has_database` = no; `has_frontend` = no; `frontend_mode` = "none"; `n_auth_flows` = 0. NEVER silently assume — every filled-in value carries a recorded rationale.

substep.title: Topology + quality-gate resolution
substep.action: Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=convention` (append `--lang=<LANG> --framework=<FRAMEWORK>` when those signals are known, to override filesystem detection). It prints one convention rule-file path per line for THIS project's stack. Read EVERY printed file.

Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=code-rules` (same `--lang`/`--framework` flags when known). It prints the project's code-rule file paths. Read EVERY printed file — these define the catalogue Stage 1 checks the diff against.

From the loaded convention packs, resolve the TOPOLOGY: SOURCE ROOTS (where production code lives), LAYER NAMES (the architectural layering the packs declare), ROOT NAMESPACE (the project's top-level namespace/module prefix), and the QUALITY-GATE COMMANDS (ALL gate commands the packs prescribe — static-analysis, lint/style, tests, AND any further gates such as architecture/boundary, manifest/schema, and db-schema — emitted bare, host-style; the daemon wraps them at dispatch). Carry the quality-gate command list forward IN FULL — the COMPLETE resolved set, never just the static-analysis/lint/style/tests triad — it is the GATE_SET floor every emitted goal's validate renders.

check.condition: `tmux-cli rules resolve` prints no convention files (empty catalogue / greenfield)
check.action: Apply greenfield DEFAULTS and mark each as a default (not a detected value): SOURCE ROOTS = ["src"]; LAYER NAMES = none declared; ROOT NAMESPACE = empty; QUALITY-GATE COMMANDS = none. With no catalogue and no quality gates there is nothing to enforce — record this; Stage 1 will report "no resolved rules / skeleton topology" and likely emit zero goals.

substep.rule: NEVER hardcode a source root, layer name, root namespace, quality-gate command, port, runner name, lang, or framework. Every such value is either resolved from the loaded packs/manifests above or is an explicitly-flagged greenfield default. No project name appears anywhere.

substep.title: Emit resolved capability + topology context block
substep.action: Compose ONE structured context block carrying everything the later stages consume:
substep.emit: • SCOPE: the raw $ARGUMENTS scope token (empty → working-tree default) for Stage 1 to resolve into `tmux-cli rules check` flags. • SIGNALS: lang, framework, run_target, has_database, has_frontend, frontend_mode, n_auth_flows — each with its source (detected / greenfield-default) and rationale for any filled-in unknown. • TOPOLOGY: source roots, layer names, root namespace, and the FULL quality-gate command list (static-analysis, lint/style, tests, plus any architecture/boundary, manifest/schema, and db-schema gates the packs prescribe) — each tagged detected-from-packs vs greenfield-default. • CODE RULES: the resolved code-rule file paths (the catalogue Stage 1 checks against), or "none". • SETTINGS: relevant .tmux-cli/setting.yaml values incl. `cr.auto_approve` / `feature.auto_approve`, or "absent".
substep.action: Hand this context block to Stage 1. Proceed to Stage 1.

execution-rules.rule: PORTABILITY: every signal / topology / quality-gate value is resolved from `tmux-cli rules resolve` / `--signals` / manifests — never hardcoded; greenfield skeleton values are flagged defaults only; no project name anywhere.

AUTONOMOUS: $ARGUMENTS is the optional scope, not a required description; empty selects the working-tree default. Resolve every unknown signal to its greenfield default with a recorded rationale; never block on the user (cr-apply-fixes is interactive only at the Stage 2 confirm gate).
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected results (a greenfield empty catalogue, a missing setting.yaml).

llm.critical-rules: • AUTONOMOUS stage — $ARGUMENTS is the OPTIONAL scope (empty → working-tree default); resolve every unknown signal to its greenfield default with a recorded rationale; never block on the user. • PORTABILITY IS MANDATORY — every signal / topology / quality-gate command is RESOLVED from `tmux-cli rules resolve` / `--signals` / manifests; no hardcoded source root, layer, namespace, runner, or project name. • Carry the FULL quality-gate command list forward (the COMPLETE resolved set — static-analysis, lint/style, tests, and architecture/manifest/db-schema where the packs prescribe them) — it is the GATE_SET floor every emitted goal's validate renders downstream. • Emit ONE resolved context block (SCOPE + SIGNALS + TOPOLOGY + CODE RULES + SETTINGS) for Stage 1; an empty catalogue / greenfield topology is an informational note, never a crash.
